title: potentilla rupestris var. mollis
intro: Potentilla rupestris var. mollis, also known as Rock Cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of tannins and flavonoids. The medicinal properties of Potentilla rupestris var. mollis have been found to provide various health benefits, including reducing swelling and pain, as well as exhibiting antibacterial and antiviral activities. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in the form of ointments and salves to aid in wound healing and skin issues.
